name: Mexico
welcome: ¡Hola! Welcome to Mexico — land of ancient pyramids, colourful celebrations, and amazing tacos!
summary: Mexico is a vibrant country in southern North America, full of colour, music, and flavour.
It was home to the mighty Aztec and Maya civilisations, who built enormous pyramids.
Mexico is famous for its delicious food, lively festivals, and beautiful beaches.

LOCATION.intro: Let's find Mexico — it is the land between two great oceans!

items.title: Mexico in North America
narration: Mexico is in southern North America, with the USA to its north.
It has coasts on both the Pacific Ocean and the Gulf of Mexico.
Mexico City, the capital, is one of the largest cities in the world!

LANDMARKS.intro: Mexico has ancient pyramids and incredible ruins!

items.title: Chichén Itzá
narration: Chichén Itzá is a giant pyramid built by the ancient Maya people over 1,000 years ago!
The pyramid has exactly 365 steps — one for every day of the year.
It is one of the New Seven Wonders of the World!

items.title: Teotihuacán
narration: Teotihuacán is an ancient city near Mexico City with massive pyramids!
The Pyramid of the Sun is so large you can see it from kilometres away.
A whole city of over 100,000 people lived here 2,000 years ago.

FOOD.intro: Mexican food is one of the most delicious and colourful in the world!

items.title: Tacos
narration: Tacos are Mexico's most famous food and are eaten for breakfast, lunch, and dinner!
A soft corn tortilla is filled with meat, beans, cheese, salsa, and lime.
Street taco stands are everywhere in Mexico — the smells are incredible!

items.title: Guacamole
narration: Guacamole is a creamy dip made from mashed avocado with lime, onion, and chilli!
Avocados were first grown in Mexico thousands of years ago.
Dip a crunchy tortilla chip into guacamole — it is absolutely delicious!

items.title: Churros
narration: Churros are long, crispy, golden-fried pastries rolled in cinnamon sugar!
They are often dipped into thick, melted chocolate sauce.
One of the most popular street sweets in all of Mexico and Spain!

ANIMALS.intro: Mexico is home to remarkable and unique creatures!

items.title: Axolotl
narration: The axolotl is one of the most magical creatures in the world — it never grows up!
It keeps its feathery gills and stays in the water its whole life.
The axolotl can regrow its own legs, heart, and even parts of its brain!

items.title: Monarch Butterfly
narration: Every year, hundreds of millions of orange Monarch butterflies fly to Mexico for the winter!
They travel over 4,000 kilometres from Canada and the United States.
The forests of Mexico glow orange when they all arrive together.

FESTIVALS.intro: Mexico's festivals are bursting with colour and tradition!

items.title: Día de los Muertos
narration: Día de los Muertos means Day of the Dead — but it is actually a joyful celebration!
Families build beautiful altars decorated with marigolds and photos to honour loved ones.
People dress up in colourful skeleton costumes and dance in the streets.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Mexico has some incredible facts to discover!

items.title: Ancient Chocolate
narration: Chocolate was invented in Mexico by the ancient Maya and Aztec civilisations!
They made a bitter drink from cacao beans — the first chocolate drink in history.
Now the whole world loves chocolate — and it all started in Mexico!

items.title: Mariachi Music
narration: Mariachi bands are the heart and soul of Mexican music!
They wear fancy charro suits with big sombreros and play guitars, violins, and trumpets.
If you have a birthday in Mexico, a mariachi band might come and serenade you!
